traps: Instance configuration uses a columnFormIPM matrix — send the WRAPPED shape, NOT dotted-path keys. Shape: { columnFormIPM: { value: [{ <selectorId>: { value: '<n>' }, ... }] } }. Selector ids: 'Number of Nodes', 'Instance Type', 'Deployment Option', 'TermType', 'LeaseContractLength', 'PurchaseOption'. The literal string 'undefined' is the utilization slot key, and its inner value is itself an object: { value: { unit: '<percent>', selectedId: '%Utilized/Month' } }. The dotted-path syntax (e.g. 'columnFormIPM[0].Instance Type') is silently dropped by the pricing engine — saves succeed, lint passes, but the rehydrated estimate renders $0 because the row's content is not in the canonical shape. Verified empirically 2026-05-30 against a UI-built reference save.

storageVolume options: 'General Purpose' (gp2), 'General Purpose-GP3' (gp3), 'Provisioned IOPS' (io1), 'Provisioned IOPS-IO2' (io2), 'Magnetic'. The provisioning IOPS and gp3 IOPS/throughput fields are conditional on the storage volume choice.
Deployment Option choices: 'Single-AZ' (default, lowest cost), 'Multi-AZ', 'Multi-AZ DB cluster' (read replicas). Multi-AZ doubles DB instance cost.
RDS Extended Support pricing kicks in only when the engine version is past EOL. Set addRDSExtendedSupport=1 only if you've explicitly chosen extended-support pricing; otherwise leave at 0 (default).
